name: Controlled Demolition Theory
description: The Controlled Demolition Theory, primarily associated with the collapse of the World Trade Center towers and 7 World Trade Center on September 11, 2001, posits that these buildings were brought down not by the impact of the airplanes and subsequent fires alone, but through the use of pre-planted explosives in a controlled demolition. Proponents argue that the speed and manner of the collapses, the presence of molten metal in the debris, and eyewitness accounts of explosions suggest the official explanation is insufficient. The theory originated in the immediate aftermath of 9/11, fueled by skepticism towards the official narrative and the desire for a more complete understanding of the events. Key evidence cited often includes supposed symmetrical collapses, "squibs" of dust and debris ejected from the buildings during collapse, and the rapid onset of the collapses. Mainstream scientific and engineering consensus, as represented by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) reports, attributes the collapses to structural damage caused by the airplane impacts and ensuing fires, which weakened the steel support structures. NIST concluded that the fires weakened the steel, leading to a progressive collapse initiated by the sagging floors pulling inward on the exterior columns. Critics of the Controlled Demolition Theory point to the lack of credible evidence supporting the presence of explosives, the implausibility of planting explosives undetected in such secure buildings, and the overwhelming evidence supporting fire-induced structural failure. The theory continues to be a subject of debate and is often found in conspiracy theory circles.
subtitle: Explosives and the 9/11 WTC Collapses

claims.claim: The collapses were too rapid and symmetrical to be caused by fire alone.
claims.evidence: Videos showing the buildings collapsing straight down at near free-fall speed; Witness accounts of explosions.
claims.criticism: NIST explains the collapses as progressive failures initiating at the impact zones and propagating downwards. Symmetry explained by structural weaknesses caused by fire and impact damage.

claims.claim: Molten metal was found in the debris, indicating the use of thermite or thermate.
claims.evidence: Eyewitness accounts and photographic evidence of molten metal in the rubble.
claims.criticism: NIST attributes the molten metal to the burning of various materials within the buildings, including aluminum, and the high temperatures generated by the prolonged fires. No evidence of thermite/thermate residue was found.

claims.claim: "Squibs" of dust and debris were ejected from the buildings during the collapse, indicating explosive charges.
claims.evidence: Video footage showing puffs of dust and debris emanating from the buildings before and during collapse.
claims.criticism: NIST explains these "squibs" as being caused by air and debris being forced out of the building as the floors collapsed sequentially, creating pressure waves that expelled material from the windows.

keyFigures.name: David Ray Griffin

keyFigures.name: Steven E. Jones
keyFigures.role: Physicist who investigated alleged thermite residues

keyFigures.name: Shyam Sunder
keyFigures.role: Lead investigator of the NIST WTC investigation

timeline.event: The World Trade Center towers and 7 WTC collapse.
First formulations of the Controlled Demolition Theory emerge.
NIST releases its final report on the collapse of the WTC towers.
NIST releases its final report on the collapse of WTC 7.
